checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=30511954 (2013-09-30) jumped to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=36807099 (2013-12-31), a ~20.7% single-quarter increase after years of near-flat balances (e.g., IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=30495485 (2010-12-31))
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet, intangible assets footnote

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=67196245 (2012-12-31) grew to InventoryNet=78171670 (2013-12-31), +16.3%, while SalesRevenueNet grew only +7.2% (SalesRevenueNet=228537050 (2012) to SalesRevenueNet=244870731 (2013))
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=1211816 (2010-12-31) versus AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=1042653 (2014-12-31) essentially flat-to-declining while SalesRevenueNet grew from 252792263 (2010) to 286242169 (2014), +13.2%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 balance sheet and income statement, five-year comparison

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: form=10-Q/A filing_date=2012-08-06 amending the 2011-06-30 period (originally reported over a year earlier), and form=10-Q/A filing_date=2012-10-31 amending the 2011-09-30 period
evidence.location: Filing chronology: 10-Q/A filings for FY2011 Q2 and Q3

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-03-04 (accession 0001144204-13-012622) followed one day later by a 10-K/A filed 2013-03-05
evidence.location: Filing chronology, FY2012 10-K and immediate amendment

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided from 2009 through 2015-06-30 for Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, Inventory, Receivables, and Cash Flow items, spanning multiple fiscal years (e.g., SalesRevenueNet annual series 2009-2014 and quarterly through Q2 2015)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible aggressive channel loading / delayed markdown recognition causing inventory and receivables to build faster than sales in FY2013, allowing reported net income to stay positive while cash generation turned negative, consistent with working-capital-driven earnings management rather than organic cash earnings.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 cash flow and income statement

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=78171670 (2013-12-31) up from InventoryNet=67196245 (2012-12-31), +16.3%, vs SalesRevenueNet growth of only +7.2% over the same period
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 balance sheet and income statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Restatement of previously issued Q2/Q3 2011 financial statements via 10-Q/A filings roughly 13-14 months after original filing suggests correction of prior period errors, indicating a history of reporting inaccuracies that raises the likelihood of undetected misstatements in adjacent periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=13000426 (2011-06-30) filed 2012-08-06 as form 10-Q/A (accession 0001144204-12-042987), restating a period originally due over a year prior

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reserve/accrual balances that failed to grow with revenue (e.g., accrued liabilities) combined with declining gross margin and rising inventory suggest potential under-reserving for inventory obsolescence, returns, or other liabilities, which would understate expenses and overstate net income/assets.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=1211816 (2010-12-31) vs AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=1042653 (2014-12-31), roughly flat despite SalesRevenueNet growing from 252792263 (2010) to 286242169 (2014)

Gross margin fell from GrossProfit=88300429/SalesRevenueNet=239968770 (2011, ~36.8%) to GrossProfit=96360725/SalesRevenueNet=286242169 (2014, ~33.7%) coincident with rising InventoryNet=85237042 (2014-12-31)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 income statement and balance sheet

top_signals: FY2013 net income positive (NetIncomeLoss=7372756) while operating cash flow was negative (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-2453218), a stark earnings-cash divergence
Prior-period 10-Q/A restatements for Q2 and Q3 2011 filed 13-14 months late, indicating a documented history of financial statement corrections

Inventory growth (InventoryNet +16.3% in 2013) outpacing revenue growth (+7.2%) alongside multi-year gross margin compression (36.8% in 2011 to 33.7% in 2014), suggesting possible inventory/obsolescence reserve inadequacy
Unusually smooth net income trajectory relative to highly volatile operating cash flow across 2010-2014
Accrued liabilities essentially flat (1.21M to 1.04M) despite 13%+ revenue growth over the same period
A sudden ~20.7% single-quarter jump in intangible assets (Q3 2013 to Q4 2013) after years of stability, warranting further inquiry into the underlying transaction
